'''Jacob Douglas''' (born 3 March 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Whitehaven RLFC|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship]], as well as [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in [[RFL League One]] and in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Douglas has also played on loan at the [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|second tier]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/jacob-douglas-56898.html|title=Jacob DOUGLAS - Player statistics - It's rugby|website=www.itsrugby.co.uk}}</ref>

==Background==
Douglas was born in [[Oldham, Greater Manchester]], England. 

He played for the [[Waterhead Warriors]] as a junior.<ref name="LRLbackgroun">{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/nextgen-jacob-douglas-wigan-warriors|title=NextGen: Meet the 'born finisher' at Wigan Warriors tipped for bright future|first=Ross|last=Heppenstall|date=July 1, 2024|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

In 2019, he joined the Warriors’ Scholarship and Academy system, and into the Wigan first team ahead of the 2023 season.<ref name="LRLbackgroun" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/jacob-climbs-the-ladder-at-wigan/|title=Jacob climbs the ladder at Wigan – Total Rugby League}}</ref>

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Douglas made his professional debut in February 2023 on loan at [[Whitehaven RLFC|Whitehaven]] against the [[London Broncos]] in the [[2023 RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c3rj0dq34w3o|title=Jacob Douglas: St Helens sign ex-Wigan Warriors winger on three-year deal|date=November 12, 2025|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In June 2024 he made his [[Wigan Warriors]] debut in the [[Super League]] against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="LRLbackgroun" />

===St Helens===
Douglas joined arch rivals St Helens on a three-year deal following the conclusion of his [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] deal at the end of the [[2025 Super League season]].<ref name="auto"/><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/st-helens-swoop-sign-wigan-1134478|title=St Helens swoop to sign Wigan Warriors winger|first=Matthew|last=Shaw|date=May 6, 2025|website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/st-helens-recruit-opens-up-on-life-after-leaving-wigan-warriors-111359/|title=St Helens recruit opens up on life after leaving Wigan Warriors|first=Will|last=Jackson|date=December 27, 2025}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (loan)===
On 25 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on season-long loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Mar 2026|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=St Helens youngster makes cross-Super League loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-youngster-makes-cross-super-league-loan-switch}}</ref>

On 11 May 2026 it was reported that [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] had recalled Douglas from his loan spell.

===Salford RLFC (loan)===
On 21 May 2026 it was reported that he had joined [[Salford RLFC|Salford]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 May 2026|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=St Helens pair make immediate Championship loan switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-pair-make-immediate-championship-loan-switch}}</ref>
